page: Microsoft Azure D2ds_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DDSv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 1 physical Intel Xeon 8272CL core with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a base speed of 2.6 GHz and is equipped with a 35 MB L3 cache. The system includes 8.0 GB of system memory, translating to 4.0 GB per core, and is bundled with a 77 GB local NVMe SSD storage drive while supporting Premium Storage.

Benchmark results indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average category. However, multi-core performance is limited, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores placing in the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, registering 11.82 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 9.21 GB/sec for writes. Cryptographic and compression tasks perform reasonably well in single-threaded scenarios, but database operations and static web serving throughput remain low.

Given its hardware profile, the D2ds_v4 offers a balanced resource density for entry-level general-purpose workloads. The inclusion of local SSD storage provides a qualitative advantage for temporary data caching without requiring external disk attachments. However, the low multi-core scaling and constrained memory bandwidth make it less suitable for heavy database operations, software compilation, or parallel processing. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded application hosting, development environments, and basic administrative tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D2ds_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DDSv4 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It provides 2 dedicated vCPUs from a single physical core, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 77 GB of local SSD storage. The processor features a 35 MB L3 cache and supports AVX-512 vector instructions. While single-core benchmarks for CPU and compression tasks show average performance, multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth are limited, placing in the bottom 10% of tested systems. This virtual machine is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring local scratch space but modest compute power. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, development environments, and single-threaded application hosting.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2ds_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 77 GB of local SSD storage, suitable for lightweight workloads.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel processing and local SSD storage for entry-level workloads.
bullet_points: 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor at 2.6 GHz
8.0 GB of system memory and 77 GB of local SSD storage
Average single-core performance for basic computing and compression tasks
Constrained multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth under heavy loads
Best suited for lightweight web serving, development, and single-threaded applications
